inline_comments.message: I suggest to add the new logic in the ModelOperation class (or a new class ModelDefaultImportsOperation), leaving only the entrypoint method addTypesToImport that will call the ModelOperation.addTypesToImport.
this is for all types, so I would put something like typeYaml or typesYaml
I believe you could do everything in one method "buildContent", iterating through the new types "dataTypeYml", adding the new ones to the originalContent, and replacing the existing ones.
to make the code clear, just return a list instead of receiving one as argument.
suggest to change to a more meaningful name, like existingImportYamlMap
raw type use of Iterator, better type it to Iterator<Entry<String, Object>>

this method "Removes the entry for the specified key only if it is currently mapped to the specified value.". If the values are exactly the same, it should not be removed.

As there is no implemented way to compare if a type is different from the other, I would recommend to always remove.
I believe you could convert the whole map back to yaml, using the same tactic as you used to convert an entry "new YamlUtil().objectToYaml(hashMap);"
same comments related to raw types usage, and foreach
include is too wide, I would recommend to use includeToModelDefaultImports
include is too wide, I would recommend to use includeToModelDefaultImports
suggest to change to includeToModelImport or includeToModelDefaultImports

no, cannot use same tactic, bcz it produces unwanted result (include 'before', 'after', etc)
it is LinkedHashMap, that is why
